priorities.title: Unescaped user data in innerHTML — XSS risk
priorities.summary: room.id, entry.id, entry.name interpolated raw into innerHTML
priorities.note: room-layout-editor.html:4513–4543. escapeHtml() defined at line 3032 but not used in these locations. XSS via malformed project file.
priorities.solution: Wrap all interpolated values in escapeHtml() at affected lines
priorities.proposed_solution: Audit all innerHTML assignments in room-layout-editor.html and produce a flagged report listing every unsafe interpolation site. Hand the report to Cybersecurity and Development with exact line references so they can wrap each value in the existing escapeHtml() helper.

priorities.title: Undefined CSS variable --text-dim causes silent render failure
priorities.summary: product-shell.css:585 references var(--text-dim) which is not defined
priorities.note: .marketing-docs-linkout inherits wrong color. No visible error in devtools unless inspecting.
priorities.solution: Define --text-dim in :root token block or replace with var(--muted)
priorities.proposed_solution: Scan the full CSS token block in product-shell.css for undefined variables and document each instance. Deliver a short findings note to Development recommending either adding --text-dim to the :root block or replacing its uses with var(--muted), whichever aligns with Design intent.

priorities.title: No global :focus-visible rule in room-layout-editor.html
priorities.summary: All native interactive elements lack keyboard focus indicators
priorities.note: Mandatory per CLAUDE.md new-page checklist. One CSS rule at :root level fixes all elements.
priorities.solution: Add :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid rgba(0,232,200,0.35); outline-offset: 2px; } to room-layout-editor.html
priorities.proposed_solution: Verify the absence of a :focus-visible rule across the room editor CSS and confirm no per-element workaround fills the gap. Write a one-line findings note for Development with the exact CSS rule to add, per the CLAUDE.md checklist requirement.

opportunities.title: Passive income market scan delivered — pick first revenue experiment
opportunities.summary: Library report covers itch.io asset economics, AI and education products, and platform fees
opportunities.note: Full findings at research/library/findings/passive-income-market-scan-2026-04-01.md. Finance should model net revenue at a few price points; founder chooses one first SKU (small pack vs. short guide).
opportunities.solution: Orchestrator runs decision mode; founder approves first experiment and time budget

opportunities.title: MCPP & Tool Research
opportunities.note: Research applicable MCP tools for our projects, and research use of deterministic tooling. brainstorm initiatives to make use of deterministic tooling in our AI workflows i.e., to reduce token use and to increase overall determinism and effectiveness of agents by reducing their workload.

actions.name: Codebase Scan
actions.description: Codebase-wide scan for consistency gaps, accessibility issues, risky patterns, and dead code across all tool files.
actions.trigger: When a new feature lands or any tool file is significantly changed

actions.name: Audit
actions.description: Focused audit of a specific file, component, or subsystem — produces a findings report with severity ratings and handoff notes for the owning agent.
actions.trigger: When a specific area is flagged as a concern by any agent or the founder

actions.name: Library Scan
actions.description: Review and update the research library under research/library/ — check for stale entries, duplicate findings, and gaps against the current codebase state.
actions.trigger: Before any major release or after a cluster of new findings are logged

actions.name: Market Scan
actions.description: Competitive and market intelligence scan — surveys comparable tools, platforms, or revenue opportunities and writes findings into the research library.
actions.trigger: When founder or Orchestration requests market context for a product or revenue decision

actions.name: Deep Research
actions.description: In-depth investigation of a specific technical or strategic question — broader scope than a single-file audit, may span multiple sources and produce a long-form report.
actions.trigger: When a question requires more than a codebase scan to answer (e.g. architecture decisions, technology choices, external platform analysis)
